opportunities.rationale: March FOMC is the key macro risk event of the week: markets price no rate change, so Powell’s tone on oil-driven inflation and the dot plot will drive direction for indices, USD and gold. Reduce directional risk 1–2 days before; best edge is trading the post-statement breakout or fade once the initial spike settles.

PPI hits just hours before the FOMC decision and is the final read on upstream inflation. A material upside surprise reinforces higher-for-longer expectations, pressuring indices and supporting USD; a soft print eases pressure and favours a relief bid in risk assets. Directional bias should be set at the print, not pre-positioned.

opportunities.event_name: European Central Bank Rate Decision & Press Conference
opportunities.rationale: ECB meets the day after FOMC in a week already dense with central-bank decisions. With Eurozone growth soft and policy rates near restrictive territory, any surprise in guidance or Lagarde’s tone could trigger outsized moves in EUR and Eurozone indices given already elevated global volatility.

opportunities.event_name: US-Iran Conflict & Strait of Hormuz Disruption
opportunities.rationale: War in Iran and threats to close the Strait of Hormuz have sharply disrupted tanker traffic and pushed global oil benchmarks markedly higher, lifting energy volatility, war-risk premiums and recession concerns. Any escalation or credible de-escalation headline can move oil, gold and indices several percent intraday; de-risk into major headlines and look to fade overreactions where liquidity allows.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on pullback to 5025-5080 zone and RSI oversold on 1h; invalidated if daily close below 4925.
opportunities.rationale: Gold remains in a bullish channel, supported by safe-haven demand from the Iran conflict and energy-driven inflation risk. A controlled pullback into the 5025 area offers a defined-risk entry with upside toward the prior swing high near 5445, provided daily closes hold above key support.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Long on dip to 80-82 on any de-escalation move that fails to break below 77.5; exit if a durable ceasefire is confirmed.
opportunities.rationale: WTI has surged on shipping disruptions and war risk in the Gulf; a pullback to the 80–82 area offers a chance to re-enter the uptrend with a stop below prior support. Thesis is invalidated by a durable ceasefire or large OPEC+ supply response that caps prices.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on intraday dip to 6720-6760 if price holds above 6700 as FOMC and PPI volatility is absorbed; stand aside if selling accelerates through 6640.
opportunities.rationale: US500 is trading in the high 6,600s after an in-line 2.4% CPI print, with support near the 200-day area and resistance around 6,880–7,000. A brief relief bounce remains possible if PPI and FOMC do not deliver fresh hawkish surprises, but holding periods should be short given macro and war uncertainty.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on intraday dip to 24600-24800 only if price holds above 24200 after PPI and FOMC; skip the trade if AI and high-beta tech remain under broad pressure.
opportunities.rationale: NAS100 remains more sensitive to rate expectations and AI positioning than US500. With macro volatility elevated and earnings dispersion high, conviction in a clean relief rally is low; only trade if buyers clearly defend key support and VIX begins to retreat.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on dips toward 23500-23800 if US–Iran tensions show credible signs of easing and ECB communication is not markedly more hawkish; exit on renewed escalation headlines.
opportunities.rationale: The DAX has rebounded from war lows but remains sensitive to both energy prices and ECB policy. Any convincing de-escalation in the Gulf combined with benign ECB messaging could fuel a sharp relief rally toward recent highs; renewed tension or hawkish surprises would invalidate the setup.
